ops.title: yasyf owns the whole yasyf/* Go ecosystem — cross-importing his own modules is fine (licenses moot), other plans are context not constraints, and he prefers automatic zero-config enablement
ops.body:
When designing cc-present/cc-interact/synckit/cc-runtime work: yasyf owns every repo in the ecosystem, so never treat licensing between his own repos (e.g. synckit's PolyForm NC) as a blocker to importing one from another — asking about it read as "don't be stupid". Plans from other sessions/repos (e.g. the cc-runtime "prancy-newt" plan) are shared as *context*, not locked constraints; the current session decides from first principles and may supersede them.

**Why:** I presented prancy-newt's "copy patterns, don't import synckit" as a locked decision and offered license-driven options; the user corrected both framings ("nothing is locked, you are deciding", "we own both why do we care about the license").

**How to apply:** Cross-repo changes and version bumps across yasyf/* are routine — plan them as part of the fix. When offering enablement options for his own tooling, note he picked automatic-when-present over explicit opt-in for synckit trust in cc-present. Related: [[plugin-not-cached-scope-fix]].
